properties.name: Koshi-Kosi System
properties.control_class: Shared Himalayan basin routed through Nepal
properties.note: Major Nepal-origin contributor to the Ganges system, with strong monsoon dominance and high downstream sediment significance.

properties.name: Gandaki-Narayani-Gandak System
properties.note: Nepal's strongest measured border discharge among the main basins in the 2024 basin plans.

properties.name: Karnali-Ghaghara System
properties.note: Large western Himalayan system with substantial undeveloped power potential relative to its strategic value.

properties.name: Mahakali-Sharda System
properties.control_class: Shared Himalayan basin routed through Nepal
properties.note: Border-river system with strong geopolitical importance, but the public source stack here did not yield a clean annual discharge figure.

properties.name: Yamuna Basin
properties.note: Large northern Indian basin with major downstream relevance but no Nepal-origin control over its headwaters.

properties.name: Ramganga Basin
properties.note: Useful Himalayan comparison basin on the Indian side, showing that not every mountain-fed Gangetic tributary runs through Nepal.

properties.name: Gomti Basin
properties.note: Plains-origin comparison basin with a very different gradient and leverage profile from Nepal-linked Himalayan systems.

properties.name: Son Basin
properties.note: Large non-Nepal basin useful for comparing scale with Nepal-linked tributary systems entering the Ganges plain from the north.
